title: docs/tutorials: add tutorial on GitLab CI and caching
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read and understood our contributing guidelines
(https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) as well as ensuring
that all your commits are signed with `git commit -s`.
-->

#### What type of PR is this?

<!--
Please label this pull request according to what type of issue you are
addressing, especially if this is a release targeted pull request.

Uncomment only one `/kind <>` line, hit enter to put that in a new line, and
remove leading whitespace from that line:
-->

/kind documentation

#### What this PR does / why we need it:
Explain how to do caching with CI

#### How to verify it
Follow tutorial and see if it works

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

Fixes #2954

#### Special notes for your reviewer:
https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/2954#issuecomment-1602390693

I had to apply the workaround in https://github.com/containers/container-selinux/issues/257 to my runner before this would work, so I've made it draft until that issue is resolved (it's probably a PEBCAK but I couldn't figure it out).

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *penn5* **Once this PR has been reviewed and has the lgtm label**, please assign [nalind](https://github.com/nalind) for approval. For more information see [the Kubernetes Code Review Process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process). The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

You have to sign your commits.
git commit -a --amend -s
git push --force
Will do. If I haven't by tomorrow, it's because I forgot, so please feel free to ping me!

Can you cover the relation with the Containerfile instruction `RUN`, for example:

```Containerfile
RUN \
  --mount=type=cache,target=/var/cache/apk \
  apk add curl
```

And `podman-build`'s `--cache-from`, `--cache-to`?
This doesn't work. I'll open an issue to make a relevant feature in buildah.
